## 7. Per-serial interactive HTML exports

Writes one self-contained `.html` file per serial number into the
`OUTPUT_HTML_DIR` folder. Each file plots that serial's **10V** and **300V**
gain error together with the chamber temperature and humidity recorded during

%{x}
%{y:.2f} %' )) sfig.update_layout( title=f'PXIe-4080 Gain Error (10V and 300V) and Chamber Conditions - Serial {ser}', width=1400, height=800, hovermode='x unified', xaxis=dict(title='Time', domain=[0.0, 0.88]), yaxis=dict(title='Gain Error - PPM'), yaxis2=dict(title=dict(text='Avg Chamber Temperature (°C)', font=dict(color='red')), tickfont=dict(color='red'), overlaying='y', side='right'), yaxis3=dict(title=dict(text='Avg Chamber RH (%)', font=dict(color='green')), tickfont=dict(color='green'), overlaying='y', side='right', position=0.96), legend=dict(x=0, y=1, bgcolor='rgba(255,255,255,0.6)') ) out_path = os.path.join(OUTPUT_HTML_DIR, f'gain_error_{_safe_filename(ser)}.html') sfig.write_html(out_path, include_plotlyjs=True, full_html=True) written.append(out_path) print(f"Wrote {len(written)} per-serial HTML file(s) to '{OUTPUT_HTML_DIR}':") for path in written: print(f" {path}")
